prevalence.source: WHO / ECDC rare disease reporting (where applicable)

riskFactors: origin or long-term stay in endemic countries
delayed diagnosis due to low clinician familiarity
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with confirmatory biopsy/smear when needed; specialist infectious disease/dermatology referral

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High cure rates with MDT when diagnosed; disability prevention depends on early recognition

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis typically in specialist centers with pathology support
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: WHO MDT via specialist prescription; monitoring for reactions and neuropathy
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low clinical suspicion causing delayed diagnosis in some cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High access to specialist care and diagnostics once suspected
